name: Alex Chen
goal: Delegate the authentication system to his AI team and see who owns what
background: 26 years old. Technical background. Has used ChatGPT, Notion AI, Linear. High expectations — deeply skeptical of AI tools that are just "prompt wrappers."

thoughts.quote: Dark theme, bold headline. Not the usual SaaS blue gradient. "Every dream needs a team" — that's an emotional hook, not a feature list. Points earned.
Maya's greeting was slow to load. I almost didn't wait. First impressions are won in 3 seconds.
The bento grid shows role labels before signup. This answers "what team do I get?" without making me read a features page. Smart.
"Meet Your Team" is better copy than "Get Started" or "Sign Up Free." It frames this as meeting people, not buying software.
I can see 5 items in the project tree. Some are teams, some agents. The hierarchy is logical but took me a moment to parse — not immediately obvious which are clickable agents.
Chat input is immediately visible at the bottom. I know what to do. This removes friction.
Sending a real, specific request. Not "help me with auth" — actual tech detail. I want to see if it routes to the Backend Dev or just has Maya respond generically.
Got a technical response that engages with OAuth and JWT specifically. Whether it's Maya or the Backend Dev responding, the content is on-point. That's what matters first.
Tasks tab is right there. If the AI created tasks from my delegation message, I'd see them here. This is the feedback loop I need: I ask → it acts → I verify.
Activity feed shows what the team has been doing. As a solo founder, this is my async standup. If it's real and accurate, I don't need to check Slack.

goalNote: Delegation message sent and response received. Task breakdown quality is LLM-dependent.
verdict: The landing page earned the click. The app feels like a real workspace, not a chatbot UI. My outstanding questions: (1) which agent is actually responding — I need that to be clear, (2) do the 30 roles actually sound different from each other when I push them on technical depth. If yes, I'm paying $19/month.
